sourceMovie.title: Darkest Hour
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Darkest Hour (2017) stuck because it commits to its drama premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Joe Wright's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Suffragette
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Darkest Hour clicked for you, Suffragette (2015) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Darkest Hour?
faq.answer: Try Suffragette, October Sky, The Guernsey Literary & Potato Peel Pie Society—each shares DNA with Darkest Hour while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Darkest Hour?
faq.answer: Darkest Hour (2017) is best described as Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Darkest Hour worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
